title: The Wayne Brady Show

jokes.text: MR. HANKY MAKES MORE THAN ME.
jokes.setup: Dave complaining about poor compensation for his show
jokes.punchline: Comparing his salary unfavorably to a fictional South Park poop character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison that highlights entertainment industry inequities. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: HEH-HEH, REPLACEABLE? YOU GONNA REPLACE ME? DAVE CHAPPELLE OF CHAPPELLE'S SHOW? I'D LIKE TO SEE THAT.
jokes.setup: Executive says Dave is replaceable
jokes.punchline: Dave's cocky challenge that sets up the entire sketch premise
jokes.explanation: Meta-textual brilliance - Dave's arrogance becomes the setup for Wayne literally replacing him. The dramatic irony makes this exceptional.

jokes.text: ♪ BRADY SHOW THE BRADY SHOW ♪ ♪ NOT DAVE'S SHOW ♪ ♪ NOT NO MO' ♪
jokes.setup: Theme song parody replacing Dave with Wayne Brady
jokes.punchline: The dismissive 'NOT NO MO'' line delivered in sing-song
jokes.explanation: Effective parody with good wordplay, though the concept is fairly straightforward.

jokes.text: AND NOTHING MAKES ME HAPPIER THAN TO BE ABLE TO TAKE ANOTHER BLACK ACTOR'S JOB.
jokes.setup: Wayne talking about limited opportunities for Black actors in Hollywood
jokes.punchline: The brutal honesty about taking pleasure in another's professional demise
jokes.explanation: Sharply subversive joke that cuts through Hollywood platitudes with uncomfortable truth. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: BUT NOT IN THAT WAY, DUDE.
jokes.setup: Wayne saying he'll give flowers to someone
jokes.punchline: Quick clarification to avoid homosexual implication
jokes.explanation: Cheap homophobic humor that relies on outdated stereotypes rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: HI, I'M TYRONE BIGGUMS. I HEARD THAT I COULD WIN A LOT OF MONEY AND I GET A PIG-TESTICLE DINNER WITH ALL THE FIXINS. GOTTA PLAY TO WIN!
jokes.setup: Introduction of contestants on Fear Factor parody
jokes.punchline: Crack addict excited about disgusting food as much as prize money
jokes.explanation: Perfect character introduction that immediately establishes Tyrone's delusional priorities and twisted perspective.

jokes.text: ONE OF THEM TRIED TO CRAWL IN MY MOUTH!
jokes.setup: Rita lying in coffin covered with worms and bugs
jokes.punchline: Her horrified reaction to the creatures
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor typical of Fear Factor parodies. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: LEMME JUST HELP YOU OUT, GET SOME ON HIM. THERE YOU GO. LOOK AT 'EM SPREADIN', THEY SPREADIN'.
jokes.setup: Jeff lying in the bug-filled coffin
jokes.punchline: Tyrone helpfully adding more bugs while providing enthusiastic commentary
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Tyrone's helpful but twisted nature. The commentary adds personality to standard physical comedy.

jokes.text: I'M GONNA TELL YOU SOMETHING ABOUT ME, JOE ROGAN, THAT YOU MIGHT NOT KNOW. I SMOKE ROCKS.
jokes.setup: Tyrone about to reveal something personal before his turn
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of crack addiction as if it's relevant information
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment. Perfect deadpan delivery of shocking information as casual small talk. Peak Chappelle absurdism.

jokes.text: JOE ROGAN, IF IT'S ALL THE SAME TO YOU, I'D LIKE TO FINISH READIN' MY NEWSPAPER. I HAVEN'T HAD A CHANCE TO SIT STILL AND READ FOR SO LONG.
jokes.setup: Joe telling Tyrone he can come out of the bug coffin
jokes.punchline: Tyrone treating the horrific challenge as a relaxing reading opportunity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion showing how crack addiction has warped Tyrone's perception of comfort and normalcy.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW WHAT? I THINK I'M GONNA TAKE A NAP. GO AHEAD WITHOUT ME, I'LL CATCH UP.
jokes.setup: Tyrone already comfortable in the bug coffin
jokes.punchline: Deciding to sleep there and treating it like a minor delay
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurdity. Tyrone's casual attitude toward horror becomes increasingly unhinged.

jokes.text: HEY, JOE ROGAN, IS IT ALMOST LUNCHTIME? 'CAUSE I SMELL SOMEBODY COOKIN'. SMELLS LIKE... CAJUN STEAKS WITH CATFISH AND FRIED PEPPERS.
jokes.setup: Tyrone standing on burning coals
jokes.punchline: Mistaking the smell of his own burning feet for appetizing food
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant moment showing how drugs have completely disconnected Tyrone from physical reality. Original and disturbing.

jokes.text: HURRY UP, GIRL, I WANNA DO MY TURN. GRAB THAT PENIS. SUCK THEM JUICES OFF, SUCK IT!
jokes.setup: Rita struggling to eat the disgusting food challenge
jokes.punchline: Tyrone's inappropriate coaching mixing sexual language with eating contest
jokes.explanation: Relies heavily on shock value and sexual innuendo. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, JOE ROGAN, THIS IS NOT THE FIRST TIME I'VE TASTED PENIS. I'VE HAD SEVERAL IN MY LINE OF WORK.
jokes.setup: Tyrone eating the disgusting elk penis food
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of performing oral sex for drugs
jokes.explanation: Dark character revelation delivered with matter-of-fact honesty. Shocking but true to character.

jokes.text: COCAINE IS A HELL OF A DRUG.
jokes.setup: Joe's reaction to Tyrone's admission about oral sex
jokes.punchline: Tyrone's casual philosophical observation about drug effects
jokes.explanation: Instant classic line. Perfect deadpan delivery of profound understatement. Became a widely quoted meme.

jokes.text: TYRITA, I DID IT! I WON! COME DOWN ON THESE PENISES WITH ME.
jokes.setup: Tyrone winning the Fear Factor challenge
jokes.punchline: Inviting girlfriend to celebrate by sharing the disgusting food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tyrone's twisted romantic gestures, though the humor is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: AND GIVE YOU THAT ROCK YOU ALWAYS HAD YOUR EYE ON.
jokes.setup: Tyrone proposing to make an honest woman of Tyrita
jokes.punchline: The 'rock' is crack cocaine, not an engagement ring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that subverts romantic proposal tropes. Perfect character-specific humor that only Tyrone could deliver.

jokes.text: OH, TYRONE, THIS'LL GET US HIGH FOR... HOURS, I KNOW. BUT FIRST, MAY I HAVE THIS DANCE?
jokes.setup: Tyrita about to say how long the drugs will last
jokes.punchline: Tyrone's romantic interruption mixing tenderness with addiction
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that finds humor in the juxtaposition of romance and drug addiction.

jokes.text: DAVE, I'M IN THE OPERATING ROOM, UM, GETTIN' BACK SURGERY. WHERE'S THE ANESTHESIA? STOP.
jokes.setup: Dave calling Big Boi to hang out
jokes.punchline: Big Boi is literally in surgery and talking to medical staff
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor playing with the timing of Dave's call. The medical interruption adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: WE PLAYIN' ON THE MOON, BITCH. PEACE, DON'T CALL ME NO MORE.
jokes.setup: Big Boi making excuses about playing tennis with Don Cornelius
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous excuse culminating in moon tennis
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of absurd excuses. The moon location is so ridiculous it becomes funny.

jokes.text: I TOLD YOU NICK CANNON IS HILARIOUS, SO I'M DONE WITH YOU, DAD. I'M HILARIOUS TOO, REMEMBER ME? 'I'M RICK JAMES, BITCH!' REMEMBER ME?
jokes.setup: Dave's son choosing Nick Cannon over him
jokes.punchline: Dave desperately reminding his son of his famous catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Dave's career decline and the fleeting nature of comedy fame.

jokes.text: SHOULDN'TA GAVE UP YOUR SHOW, DAWG.
jokes.setup: Nick Cannon taking Dave's place with his son
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious lesson Dave should have learned
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation that drives home the consequences of Dave's decision.

jokes.text: IF IT'S MY SHOW, CAN I DO THIS? WHOO!
jokes.setup: Dave asking if it's really his show
jokes.punchline: Dave apparently doing something physical/destructive to test his control
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that works but isn't particularly innovative. Standard escalation humor.

jokes.text: BACK YO'SELF, FOOL! AWW, SHIT, IT'S WAYNE BRADY, SON! RIVERSIDE, MOTHAFUCKA!
jokes.setup: Wayne approaching what seems like a drug deal
jokes.punchline: Wayne revealing himself as a violent criminal with street credibility
jokes.explanation: Massive character subversion that completely reframes Wayne Brady's image. Shocking and brilliantly executed misdirection.

jokes.text: GODDAMN, MAN, YOU GOT A DAYTIME EMMY, NIGGA. YOU AIN'T SUPPOSED TO BE DOIN' SHIT LIKE THIS.
jokes.setup: Wayne just shot people in a drug deal
jokes.punchline: Dave's incredulous reaction about the contradiction with Wayne's Emmy
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction highlighting the absurd juxtaposition of Wayne's public image and criminal behavior.

jokes.text: HOES, DAVE. DAVE, HOES. GOOD EVENING, BITCHES.
jokes.setup: Wayne introducing Dave to prostitutes at an ATM
jokes.punchline: Wayne's polite social introduction treating it like a normal meeting
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Wayne's polite mannerisms and criminal activity. The formal introduction is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: IS WAYNE BRADY GONNA HAVE TO CHOKE A BITCH?
jokes.setup: Wayne angry at a prostitute for short-changing him
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence while referring to himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that became a cultural phenomenon. Perfect third-person threat that subverts Wayne's wholesome image completely.

jokes.text: I CAN'T TAKE THIS MONEY, MAN, THIS MONEY AIN'T CLEAN, MAN.
jokes.setup: Wayne offering Dave money from prostitution
jokes.punchline: Dave's moral objection to dirty money
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction but fairly predictable moral stance. Sets up further escalation.

jokes.text: THIS IS NOT AN OPTION, NIGGA. IF YOU DO NOT SMOKE THIS, WE HAVE A PROBLEM. AIN'T NO DAMN AFTER-SCHOOL SPECIAL. SMOKE IT.
jokes.setup: Dave refusing to smoke something Wayne offers
jokes.punchline: Wayne's menacing insistence with after-school special reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Wayne's menacing side. The after-school special reference adds clever meta-commentary.

jokes.text: YEAH, BROTHER, THAT'S P.C.P., ANGEL DUST, SHERMAN HEMSLEY, 'LOVE BOAT'...
jokes.setup: Dave asking what 'wet' means
jokes.punchline: Wayne listing PCP slang terms including nonsensical Sherman Hemsley reference
jokes.explanation: Clever mix of real and fake drug slang. Sherman Hemsley reference is absurd but fits Wayne's character.

jokes.text: WHITE PEOPLE LOVE WAYNE BRADY BECAUSE HE MAKES BRYANT GUMBEL LOOK LIKE MALCOLM X.
jokes.setup: Commentary on Wayne Brady's public persona
jokes.punchline: The extreme comparison between Bryant Gumbel and Malcolm X
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary packed into perfect joke structure. Became an instantly quotable cultural reference point.

jokes.text: ♪ THE MORNING I WAKE UP ♪ ♪ AND I PUT ON MY MAKE UP ♪ ♪ I SAY A LITTLE PRAYER FOR YOU ♪
jokes.setup: Police officer recognizing Wayne Brady
jokes.punchline: Wayne switching to his wholesome entertainer persona with singing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Wayne's dual nature. Perfect switch from criminal to entertainer.

jokes.text: THAT'S 'CAUSE SHE IS NEVER GONNA KNOW ABOUT IT, BITCH.
jokes.setup: Officer saying his mother-in-law won't believe meeting Wayne
jokes.punchline: Wayne's threatening implication that the officer won't survive
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny threat delivered with Wayne's characteristic politeness. Great tonal whiplash.

jokes.text: I MAKE BRYANT GUMBEL LOOK LIKE MALCOLM X, HUH, MOTHERFUCKER?
jokes.setup: Wayne forcing Dave to eat a sandwich
jokes.punchline: Wayne angrily referencing the earlier insult about him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Wayne heard and was hurt by the earlier insult. Great character payoff.

jokes.text: I'M WAYNE BRADY, BITCH!
jokes.setup: Wayne saying goodbye to Dave after their night together
jokes.punchline: Final assertion of his street credibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper line that became iconic. Crystallizes the entire sketch's subversion in three words.

jokes.text: I'M RICH, BIATCH!
jokes.setup: Wayne giving Dave his show back
jokes.punchline: Wayne using Dave's own catchphrase against him
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage showing Wayne's dominance by appropriating Dave's own material. Solid but not as strong as his original lines.

jokes.text: I'M RICH, BIATCH!
jokes.setup: Wayne giving the show back to Dave
jokes.punchline: Reference to another Dave Chappelle character catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to another popular Chappelle character. Works as a transitional moment back to normal.

jokes.text: I CANCELLED THREE OF MY SHOWS TO COME AND DO THIS BECAUSE THAT MEANT SO MUCH.
jokes.setup: Wayne talking about Dave calling to invite him on the show
jokes.punchline: The genuine respect and sacrifice Wayne made to appear
jokes.explanation: Sweet behind-the-scenes moment that shows the real Wayne Brady's respect for Dave. Good humanizing touch.

tmdb_overview: Dave introduces skits that were too crazy to make the cut, including footage of a trip to the Nelson Mandela bootcamp for teenagers. Dave also imagines what it would be like if the whole world was gay. Also featuring musical guests Kanye West and Mos Def.
